tasks_completed.description: Fixed stale stage-3 heading label in commands/commit.md
tasks_completed.changes: Line 221 heading changed from '(3) Mtime + literal-task-id glob (final stage).' to '(3) Mtime window + machine-readable marker predicate (final stage).' The old label inaccurately implied a literal-task-id glob; the actual stage-3 logic (line 222) globs on the basename pattern spec-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.md and applies a single content predicate grep -lF "<!-- spec-continuation-of: ${TASK_ID} -->" (a machine-readable marker), not a literal-task-id glob.
tasks_completed.rationale: AC-F1: heading must accurately describe the machine-readable marker content predicate and no longer say 'literal-task-id glob'.

git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: The stage-3 heading was written/retained describing an older approach ('literal-task-id glob') that no longer matches the implemented predicate (machine-readable marker grep on a mtime/basename-globbed candidate set).
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: Heading now names the two real predicates used in stage 3: the mtime window and the machine-readable marker content predicate, matching the body text at line 222.

dev.qa_notes: Verify commands/commit.md:221 heading no longer contains 'literal-task-id glob' and now reads '(3) Mtime window + machine-readable marker predicate (final stage).'. Confirm no test_*.sh files were touched (git diff --name-only shows only commands/commit.md as this task's change; all other diffed paths were dirty at baseline). AC-F2: zero test_*.sh changes.

self_verification.build: skipped (markdown documentation change — no build target)
self_verification.notes: Doc-only single-line heading label change. Verified line 221 now reads the corrected text and matches the body predicate at line 222. No test_*.sh files modified (AC-F2 satisfied). Other paths in git diff against baseline were already dirty per baseline_dirty_snapshot and are not part of this task's change set.
